content.text:
The current approved experience rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

### Current XP Rules
- monsters award XP based on monster level
- the XP table for monster levels 1-20 is defined
- XP is shared equally among surviving party members
- leveling up is performed at an inn
- leveling up has no gold cost
- full level-up rules will be defined separately

---

## 22. Currency and Gemstones

The current approved currency rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/currency-canon.md`

The current gemstone reference is maintained in:
- `umberhold/items/gemstones.md`

### Current Currency Rules
- Umberhold uses three coin types: Silver Rook, Gold Crown, and Platinum Stag
- Silver Rook is the base coin
- Gold Crown is worth 10 Silver Rooks
- Platinum Stag is worth 25 Gold Crowns

### Current Gemstone Rules
- gemstones are valid loot-table valuables
- gemstone values are currently tracked in Gold Crowns

---

## 23. Class Hit Dice and Equipment Rules

The current approved class hit-die and equipment rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/class-equipment-canon.md`

### Current Class Equipment Rules
- each class has a defined hit die per level
- Constitution modifier is added to each hit point roll
- every level requires a hit point roll
- there is no average hit point option
- armor restrictions are absolute
- shield restrictions are absolute
- unusable armor may still be carried but should display in red in inventory
- any class may equip any weapon
- weapon proficiency only affects whether Proficiency Bonus is added to attack rolls
- non-proficient weapon attacks do not gain Proficiency Bonus

---

## 24. Weapon Statistics

The current approved weapon table is maintained in:
- `umberhold/weapon-statistics-canon.md`

### Current Weapon Rules
- weapon entries include damage type
- weapon entries include range values from 1-3
- Quarterstaff is part of the approved weapon table
- finesse weapons may use Strength or Agility, whichever is higher

---

## 25. Approved Rules Currently Locked

The following are currently approved canon:

### Combat Turn Rules
- initiative uses `1d20 + Agility Bonus + modifiers`
- initiative order is shown in a top-left vertical tracker
- active turn holder is highlighted in red
- turn order rotates continuously after each completed turn
- each turn grants one main action
- bonus actions exist, but only when granted by a specific feature or effect
- reactions do not exist
- Defend imposes disadvantage on incoming attacks
- weapon swapping uses the full main action
- Troubadours may begin or maintain Songs with a **bonus action** when granted by class features
- extra attacks cannot be redirected if the original target dies

### Damage and Death Rules
- the current list of Umberhold damage types is locked
- vulnerability = double damage
- resistance = half damage, round down, minimum 1 damage
- immunity = no damage
- critical hit on natural 20 by default
- some features may expand critical range
- critical hits roll damage dice twice, then add bonuses
- natural 1 always misses
- death saves are not used
- 0 or fewer hit points means immediate death

### Spellcasting and Recovery Rules
- spellcasting classes start with all class cantrips and all class 1st-level spells
- cantrips do not consume spell slots
- higher-level spells are mainly acquired through adventuring, especially scrolls
- when a caster unlocks a new spell tier, they immediately learn one spell of that level
- scrolls may be cast directly if requirements are met
- scrolls may be memorized if requirements are met and the spell is not already known
- spellbooks are not physical items
- each caster has an internal spellbook interface
- no casting focus is required
- upcasting does not exist
- spells must use slots of their own level only
- Resting takes 8 hours
- Resting fully restores hit points, spell slots, and rest-based class abilities
- Resting only works in safe locations
- unsafe resting fails with a fixed message
- there is no short rest system
- spells explicitly marked as Bonus Action use the bonus action slot for the turn
- the current cantrip and 1st-level spell list is tracked in `umberhold/spells-cantrips-1st.md`

### Condition Rules
- the current approved condition list is Beguiled, Blinded, Frightened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, Silenced, Slept, and Stunned
- detailed condition behavior is tracked in `umberhold/conditions-canon.md`

### Monster Stat Block Rules
- the generic monster stat block is tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`
- monsters use Level rather than challenge rating
- current monster levels are 1-20
- monster sizes are Small, Medium, Large, Huge, and Gigantic
- Small and Medium occupy 1 combat slot
- Large occupies 2 combat slots
- Huge occupies 3 combat slots
- Gigantic occupies 4 combat slots
- unarmored monster AC starts at 10
- monster hit dice scale by size: Small 1d6, Medium 1d8, Large 1d12, Huge 2d10, Gigantic 2d20 per level
- monster proficiency bonus uses the current progression: 1-3 +2, 4-6 +3, 7-9 +4, 10-12 +5, 13-15 +6, 16-18 +7, 19-20 +8
- monsters use the same seven core attributes as player characters
- monster attributes currently range from 3-30
- monster entries include Description, damage vulnerabilities, damage resistances, damage immunities, condition immunities, Treasure Type, Comments, attacks, and XP value

### Experience Rules
- the current experience rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`
- monsters award XP based on monster level
- XP is shared equally among surviving party members
- leveling up is performed at an inn
- leveling up has no gold cost

### Currency and Gemstone Rules
- the current currency rules are tracked in `umberhold/currency-canon.md`
- the current gemstone reference is tracked in `umberhold/items/gemstones.md`
- Silver Rook is the base coin
- Gold Crown is worth 10 Silver Rooks
- Platinum Stag is worth 25 Gold Crowns

### Class Equipment Rules
- the current class hit-die and equipment rules are tracked in `umberhold/class-equipment-canon.md`
- each class has a defined hit die per level
- Constitution modifier is added to each hit point roll
- every level requires a hit point roll
- armor restrictions are absolute
- shield restrictions are absolute
- any class may equip any weapon
- non-proficient weapon attacks do not gain Proficiency Bonus

### Weapon Rules
- the current weapon table is tracked in `umberhold/weapon-statistics-canon.md`
- weapon entries include damage type
- weapon entries include range values from 1-3
- Quarterstaff is part of the approved weapon table
- finesse weapons may use Strength or Agility, whichever is higher

---

## 26. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and should be resolved in future canon passes:

### Combat Open Questions
- initiative tie-break rules
- exact turn timing for status durations
- enemy-side bonus action rules
- whether starting a new Song immediately ends the previous Song in all cases
- whether items can ever be bonus actions
- target invalidation edge cases
- mid-combat initiative changes, if any
- full player formation rules
- full enemy action economy rules

### Damage Open Questions
- interaction order between critical hits and vulnerability/resistance
- whether mixed-damage attacks split damage by type before applying modifiers
- whether damage-over-time effects can critically hit
- whether environmental damage uses the same resistance rules in all cases
- whether Holy / Unholy / Arcane / Shock have special systemic interactions beyond type tags
- whether monsters follow the exact same death rule presentation as player characters

### Spellcasting Open Questions
- exact spell-tier unlock table by class and level
- whether every caster learns the same number of spells when unlocking later tiers beyond the one free spell
- whether scrolls can ever be used by non-casters through special items or class features
- whether rare exceptions can bypass normal class spell lists
- whether some safe dungeon rest locations can be exhausted or used only once
- whether rest can ever be interrupted by scripted events in valid safe zones
- higher-level spell lists beyond 1st level

### Condition Open Questions
- whether Beguiled and Soothed should remain separate conditions or merge into one rules concept
- whether Stunned should prevent all actions instead of only applying attack/save penalties
- whether Frightened affects spell targeting or only attacks
- whether Blinded affects all targeted spell use
- whether bosses have default immunities to specific control conditions
- how cleansing/removal rules are distributed across items, spells, and services
- exact timing for condition expiration when not stated explicitly by a spell or ability

### Monster Open Questions
- whether monster hit points use only size dice or also add Constitution-derived bonuses by level
- whether alignment has any systemic effect beyond lore and spell interaction
- the full canonical skill list used by monsters and characters
- whether monster spellcasters follow player spell slot rules or use bespoke spellcasting packages

### Experience Open Questions
- the exact character XP thresholds required for levels 1-10
- whether benched roster members ever receive shared XP
- whether summoned allies or temporary NPC allies affect XP splitting
- whether story or quest XP exists in addition to monster XP
- whether escape or partial-victory outcomes grant reduced XP
- the exact level-up benefits by class and level

### Currency and Gemstone Open Questions
- whether older `gp` prices in existing notes should be universally converted to Gold Crowns
- whether shops ever display prices directly in mixed denominations
- whether gemstones can have special uses beyond sale value, such as quest, ritual, or crafting functions
- whether gemstones stack in inventory or remain individual items under no-stacking rules

### Class Equipment Open Questions
- whether first-level characters still begin with maximum hit points from their class hit die
- whether off-hand weapon proficiency follows the same rule with no exceptions
- whether class proficiencies can ever be expanded by training, items, or special features
- whether instruments for Troubadours count as weapon-category items for equipment UI purposes
- whether some relic or class effects can temporarily bypass armor restrictions

### Weapon Open Questions
- whether ammunition is abstracted or fully tracked for bows, slings, and crossbows
- whether crossbows have reload-specific handling beyond normal attack use
- whether thrown weapons will exist as a distinct category later
- whether weapon categories should be formalized for class features and item effects
- whether some named weapons gain variant properties beyond the base table

---

## 27. Time System and Daily Schedule

Umberhold uses a **discrete, action-driven time system**.

### Core Rule
One time step equals **6 seconds** of in-game time.

The following currently consume one 6-second step:
- one **successful grid movement** into a new tile
- one **full combat round** in which all combatants act

This uses **round** as the combat timekeeping unit, not an individual combatant turn.

### No Passive Time
Time advances only when the player performs an action with a defined time cost.

If the player stops acting, the world remains static.

This means there is **no real-time pressure** and no passive world simulation running in the background.

### Time of Day Presentation
The game tracks time internally, but does **not** display exact hours to the player.

Instead, the UI shows only the **current phase of the day** below the minimap.

### Day Phases
Each day is divided into **8 phases**, each lasting **3 hours**:
1. **Dead of Night** (12am-3am)
2. **Black Hours** (3am-6am)
3. **First Light** (6am-9am)
4. **High Morning** (9am-12pm)
5. **Sun at Zenith** (12pm-3pm)
6. **Fading Light** (3pm-6pm)
7. **Gloaming** (6pm-9pm)
8. **Nightfall** (9pm-12am)

Only the **phase name** is shown to the player.

### Timed Systems
This same underlying clock governs:
- spell durations
- status effects
- world interactions
- service availability

### Shop Hours
Most standard shops operate during daylight-facing phases:
- **First Light**
- **High Morning**
- **Sun at Zenith**
- **Fading Light**

**Inns** and **taverns** remain open at all hours.

The standalone reference for this system is:
- `umberhold/time-canon.md`

Implementation/data references:
- `umberhold/time-data-schema.md`
- `umberhold/schemas/time/`
- `umberhold/examples/time/`

---

## 28. First-Person Seeing Distance and Light

In first-person exploration:
- the party sees up to **3 tiles ahead** above ground during daylight-facing phases
- the party sees up to **2 tiles ahead** above ground at night

Dungeons and other adventuring locations define whether they are **lit** or **unlit**.
A lit location provides ambient visibility.
An unlit location is completely dark unless the party uses an active light source.

### Darkness Rules
In complete darkness:
- the party may still move
- they cannot see ahead
- if they walk into a solid wall, display **"Ouch."** and play an impact cue

### Approved Light Sources
The current approved exploration light sources are:
- **Light** spell
- **Hooded Lantern**
- **Torch**

These follow the rules that:
- handheld light sources must be equipped in **main hand** or **off-hand**
- only the **strongest** active light source applies
- active light overrides dungeon darkness and outdoor night darkness
- hooded lanterns provide **3 tiles** of illumination and burn **4 hours** per oil vial
- torches provide **3 tiles** of illumination and burn **1 hour**

The standalone reference for this system is:
- `umberhold/light-vision-canon.md`

---

## 29. Source Sheets

This consolidated file is currently based on:
- `umberhold/combat-turn-canon.md`
- `umberhold/damage-canon.md`
- `umberhold/spellcasting-canon.md`
- `umberhold/spells-cantrips-1st.md`
- `umberhold/class-spell-lists.md`
- `umberhold/conditions-canon.md`
- `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`
- `umberhold/currency-canon.md`
- `umberhold/items/gemstones.md`
- `umberhold/class-equipment-canon.md`
- `umberhold/weapon-statistics-canon.md`
- `umberhold/time-canon.md`
- `umberhold/light-vision-canon.md`

These may still be kept as narrower reference sheets if useful.

content.text:
# Umberhold Light and Vision Canon

This document captures the current approved rules for:
- first-person seeing distance
- darkness
- fixed dungeon lighting
- portable and magical light sources

It is intended to align exploration readability, equipment-based light rules, and timed light durations under one shared model.

---

## 1. Natural Seeing Distance

In first-person exploration, the party's unaided seeing distance depends on whether they are above ground and how much natural light is available.

### Above Ground
- during daylight-facing phases, the party sees up to **3 tiles ahead**
- during nighttime phases, the party sees up to **2 tiles ahead**

For this rule, daylight-facing outdoor phases are:
- **First Light**
- **High Morning**
- **Sun at Zenith**
- **Fading Light**

All other phases are treated as night for above-ground visibility.

### Dungeons and Other Adventuring Locations
Dungeons and other authored adventuring locations do **not** use outdoor daylight rules.

Instead, each location defines whether it is:
- **lit**
- **unlit**

A lit location provides ambient visibility through its authored presentation and does not require a party-held light source.

An unlit location is completely dark unless the party is using an active light source.

---

## 2. Darkness

In a completely dark location:
- the party may still move
- they cannot see ahead
- effective navigation requires a light source

### Collision in Darkness
If the party walks into a solid wall while in darkness:
- display the message **"Ouch."**
- play an audible impact cue

---

## 3. Light Sources

The party may create exploration light with:
- the **Light** spell
- a **Hooded Lantern**
- a **Torch**

Any other spell or item that explicitly grants illumination should follow the same strongest-source rule unless it says otherwise.

### Equip Requirement
A handheld light source must be equipped in either:
- **main hand**
- **off-hand**

to provide illumination.

### Stacking Rule
If multiple light sources are active at the same time:
- only the **strongest** illumination range applies

### Override Rule
An active light source overrides:
- dungeon darkness
- outdoor night darkness

and provides its full illumination range.

### Hard Cutoff
Beyond the current active light or visibility range, there is **complete darkness**.

---

## 4. Light Spell

The approved **Light** spell functions as a party light source in first-person exploration.

### Rules
- provides **3 tiles of illumination**
- follows the general strongest-source and darkness-override rules
- spell duration continues to be governed by the spell list entry

---

## 5. Hooded Lantern

A hooded lantern burns for **4 hours** on **one vial of oil**.

Under the shared time system, this equals **2,400 time steps**.

### Fuel Use
When a lantern is lit:
- oil is first taken automatically from the carrying character's inventory
- if the carrier has no oil, one vial is taken from a **random other party member**
- if the party has no oil, display:

> **You have no oil to fill the lantern.**

### Use Rules
- the lantern must be equipped in **main hand** or **off-hand**
- it provides **3 tiles of illumination**
- beyond that range there is complete darkness

### Expiration
When the lantern expires, display:

> **Your lantern has gone out.**

---

## 6. Torch

A torch burns for **1 hour**.

Under the shared time system, this equals **600 time steps**.

### Use Rules
- the torch must be equipped in **main hand** or **off-hand**
- it provides **3 tiles of illumination**
- beyond that range there is complete darkness
- its light should show a subtle flicker of shifting shadows within the lit radius

### Expiration
When the torch expires, display:

> **Your torch has gone out.**

---

## 7. Status

This category is **approved canon** for the current design direction.

arguments.command: grep -RIn "Oil Vial\|Flask of Oil\|Holy Symbol\|Holy Water\|Thieves' Tools\|Magnifying Glass\|Flute\|Lute\|Horn\|Drums\|Pipes" umberhold --exclude-dir=.git

content.thinking:
**Updating game mechanics**

I’m considering how to incorporate Burning and Bleeding into the current conditions summary. I think I should note that these are damage-over-time effects, with their exact damage and duration defined by sources. The user pointed out that Burning, for instance, applies 2d4 fire damage on hit and then 1d4 fire at the start of the target's next turn. I want to clarify how these DoT effects function in gameplay, as the user is curious about that.

**Defining damage conditions**

I’m thinking about how to create a general rule for Burning and Bleeding. They’re negative conditions that deal damage at the start of the affected target's next turn. The source that applies the condition dictates the damage type, amount, and duration unless stated otherwise. Generally, these conditions last only until the start-of-turn damage is resolved, and then they end. It seems to align well with existing mechanics like Thornburst, Boneboiler, and Dirty Fighting, which all follow a similar pattern!

**Clarifying damage effects**

I'm thinking there’s an open question about whether multiple separate Burning or Bleeding effects stack or refresh. The user mentioned they function as damage over time effects, which might mean each source tracks its own damage packets. Defining source-specific packets could lead to naturally separate stacking, but that's a new rule. I'm not sure if it’s necessary yet, especially since there's still uncertainty around other conditions. I might consider updating some documentation for consistency but won’t proceed if the user didn’t request it.

**Considering documentation consistency**

I think it’s important to maintain consistency in the documentation with the new canonical terms. There are only three occurrences to update, so I could revise them. For instance, in Thornburst, the target could be stated as gaining Bleeding and taking 1d4 piercing damage at the start of its next turn. Shadowman Dirty Fighting already mentions bleeding damage, so I might rephrase it to say "gains Bleeding" with an additional die. The monster boneboiler uses Bleeding too, but I should ensure that class, monster, and spell documents align with these terms.

**Updating game docs**

I'm considering how to capitalize the condition for Fighting and Thornburst. I might also need to think about how the monster Boneboiler uses this capitalized condition. Since we're adding the Bleeding condition to the conditions canon, it could improve alignment. 

I may want to create a new document for mundane equipment that includes full supplied data and current coin names. Following that, I could update the various item lists and combat-related documents accordingly, ensuring everything is coherent.

**Structuring equipment documents**

I want to avoid altering the combat-turn-canon, and the starting gear can be placed in a new document instead of class-equipment-canon. However, since the class-equipment-canon title includes equipment canon, it might still be good to reference it there. The new document could focus solely on "settlement mundane items and starting gear." 

I plan to update the conditions-canon for the Burning/Bleeding generalization as well. Finally, I should update canon.md to reference the new document, ensuring everything remains organized and coherent. Renumbering sections should help manage the flow of information.

**Considering document structure**

It seems like I have a lot to handle, but it feels doable with a full rewrite. However, I’m wondering if it’s necessary to go that far. Maybe I can just add bullets under Approved Rules and Source Sheets instead of creating a separate numbered section. That might simplify things and keep the document streamlined. **Considering document updates**

I need to clarify the document's purpose and possibly incorporate some Approved Rules. I wonder if I should create a new subsection under Currency or adjust the existing sections for clarity. It might be simpler to add the new content directly into the canon document by inserting a subsection specifically for mundane equipment. But then section 23 might need a name change, which could complicate things.

**Updating documentation structure**

I’m thinking about updating section 23 to include both the new documentation and the class-equipment canon. I could mention that current rules for mundane equipment and settlement gear are in `umberhold/mundane-equipment-canon.md`, while class hit-die and equipment restrictions are in `umberhold/class-equipment-canon.md`. This way, I can avoid renumbering. I also want to expand on the approved condition list and consider updating the item index in `umberhold/items/README.md`. However, I’m unsure if individual item files for things like holy water are necessary right now.

**Organizing equipment availability**

I’m considering item accessibility and tracking right now. Mundane items are usually easy to find, as equipment shops carry general adventuring gear alongside weapons and armor. Temples typically offer religious items too. I’m noting that weights are measured in stones, and costs will be represented in current Umberhold coin terms, like Silver Rook and Gold Crown, while food and arrows won't be tracked. Also, I'm looking at how to effectively describe the equipment shop stock.

**Detailing equipment options**

I’m outlining specific items and their availability. For instance, a Flask of Oil costs 1 Silver Rook and can fuel lanterns or be used in combat. Thieves’ Tools come at 25 Gold Crowns, weighing 1 stone, and are essential for dealing with locks and traps. A Magnifying Glass costs 100 Gold Crowns, has no weight, and provides a +2 bonus for detecting traps. 

I’m also considering including holy symbols in the temple section, like Holy Water and the Holy Symbol of Solace. Lastly, I want to define Troubadour instruments as mundane options with costs in Gold Crowns.

**Considering hurled item attacks**

I’m thinking about hurled item attacks using mundane items as weapons. The attack roll would involve a d20 plus the Agility bonus against Armor Class (AC). The range for these attacks would be 3. I should mention that this also applies to items like holy water and oil flasks, which can definitely add some strategic options in combat. It's interesting how versatile everyday items can be in a game setting!

**Clarifying damage types**

I’m thinking that maybe the damage type should align with the source's listed damage, which is generally piercing or bleed-type. However, there isn’t a distinct bleed damage type mentioned. I’m considering renaming the item "Holy Symbol of Solace" to match the temple symbols sold, aligning with the friar requirement. It would make sense to add categories or shops for clarity, noting sources like "Equipment Shop stock" and "Temple stock." I also need to update the starting gear sections in `classes/friar.md`, `classes/shadowman.md`, and `classes/troubadour.md`, since the user mentioned free starting equipment. A "Starting Equipment" subsection near the Class Summary would be helpful, detailing what each class begins with.

**Considering updates for Shadowman and spells**

I’m thinking about updating `spells-cantrips-1st.md`, but that might only be necessary for term consistency. | Sleep | HW | 1st | 3 | One enemy and up to one each side in the same rank (3 max) | Until saved or dispelled | A suffocating stillness settles in, dragging the target toward an unnatural slumber. **PIE save** or fall asleep. The target may repeat the save at the end of each turn, ending the effect on a success. The effect ends immediately if the target takes damage. |
| Shockwave | HW | 1st | 2 | One enemy and one either side | Instant | Force erupts outward with a bone-rattling crack, shuddering through flesh and armor. Deals **2d4 shock** damage. |
| Shield of Solace | F, C | 1st | 1 | One ally | Combat | A faint barrier flickers into existence, fragile yet defiant against the encroaching dark. The target gains **+2 AC**. |
| Solace's Blessing | F, C | 1st | NA | One party rank | Combat | A fragile warmth settles over the chosen rank, like a fleeting memory of safety. The chosen rank gains **+1 AC, +1 Saves, and +1 Attacks**. |
| Soothe Beast | W | 1st | 3 | One enemy (Beast only) | Until saved or dispelled | A low, steady murmur cuts through fear and fury, reaching something older than rage within the creature. **PIE save** or the target becomes **Soothed** and does not attack the party. The effect ends immediately if the target takes damage from the party. The target may repeat the save at the end of each turn, ending the effect on a success. |
| Thornburst | W | 1st | 3 | One enemy rank | Instant | The ground splits as blackened thorns and barbed roots erupt upward, tearing at flesh before sinking back into the soil. Targets take **2d4 + Casting Bonus piercing** damage. **AGI save** for half damage. If the save fails by 5 or more, the target **Bleeds**, taking **1d4 piercing** damage at the start of its next turn. |
| Vaelric's Quietus Key | HW | 1st | NA | One locked portal or container | Instant | The lock trembles as unseen forces probe its inner workings, yielding with a soft, reluctant click. Unlocks the target. |
| Vaelric's Unseen Disarmament | HW | 1st | NA | One trapped portal or container | Instant | Faint spectral fingers creep across the mechanism, silencing hidden triggers before they can spring. Removes any trap on the target. |
| Venomspit | HW | Cantrip | 3 | One enemy | Instant | A glob of hissing, corrosive venom is spat forth, eating at whatever it touches. Make a ranged spell attack: **1d20 + Proficiency Bonus + Casting Bonus vs AC**. On a hit, the target takes **2d4 acid** damage. |
| Warden's Mark | W | 1st (Bonus Action) | 3 | One enemy | Combat | A spectral brand sears itself onto the target, a silent promise that it will not escape. The target is **marked**. The Warden gains **+4 to attack rolls** and deals **one additional damage die** on each hit against the target. If cast before attacking on the same turn, attacks against the target are made with **advantage**. |

| **Attacks** | **Meat Hook:** +3 to hit, range 1, 1d6 piercing; target suffers **Bleeding** (1d4 damage at the start of its next turn). Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler lunges at (insert target) with a meat hook!* **Hurl Hot Grease:** +2 to hit, range 3, 2d4 fire damage; **recharge 6** (roll a d6 at the start of its turn and regain use on a 6); target is **Blinded** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler hurls a pot of hot grease at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Polished Finger Bone** (15%) |
| **Comments** | “Into the pot with ya!”, “Boil ’em slow, tastes better!”, “Oi, don’t waste the juices!”, “This one’ll make a fine broth!”, “Keep stirrin’, lads!”, “Boss wants this one salted!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |


[16 more lines in file. Use offset=34 to continue.]

- on a success, you harvest **1d2 doses**
- as an action, you may apply one dose to a melee weapon
- the poison is **single-use** and is expended after the first successful hit
- if you fail the harvesting check by **5 or more**, you are affected by the poison

---

### Dirty Fighting
When you hit, the target takes one additional die of damage—matching the damage die you rolled—at the start of the enemy's next turn as **bleeding** damage.

---

**Considering updates for documents**

I might update Thornburst and Dirty Fighting to capitalize and link to the condition, but I'm not sure if it's strictly necessary. I also wonder if I should update the list open question. Maybe I need to modify `currency-canon.md` if we decide on mapping sp to Silver Rook and gp to Gold Crown, but if both are used in the new doc, it may not be needed. I should assess the lengths of the files before doing full rewrites, especially since some files are shorter than others.

**Planning localized replacements**

I could use the edit function for localized replacements, but for smaller files, a full rewrite might be easier and safer. I plan to map out the necessary modifications.

For `item-list.md`, since the current content is short, I'll rewrite the entire file, adding a Cost column and including items. I need to ensure elements like the Boggart are preserved. I could also incorporate a note from the new document with proposed table rows for items like Boggart Cloak, Bone Collar, and Drums, noting their effects and details.

**Detailing item entries**

I'm planning to add item entries like the Flask of Oil and Flute into the `item-list.md`. For the Flask of Oil, which is a consumable, I note it costs 1 Silver Rook (`1 sp`), can fill a hooded lantern for 4 hours, and can be hurled for 2d4 fire damage and Burning. It will also be marked as equipment shop stock since it's automatically consumed when lighting the lantern. For the Flute, I'd indicate it costs 2 Gold Crowns (`2 gp`) and its role as an instrument.

**Considering updates to weapon documentation**

I’m thinking about whether to update `weapon-statistics-canon` to clarify that "food and arrows are not tracked." The user points out that arrows, specifically, aren't tracked, which helps partially resolve the question. I need to decide if this update should happen in `weapon-statistics-canon.md` or `canon.md`. Maybe it's fine to let this stay since the new document says arrows aren’t tracked. However, we can also clarify if sling stones and crossbow bolts are tracked, similar to arrows. I’ll inspect `weapon-statistics-canon.md` for possible edits.

**Updating inventory tracking rules**

I’m considering adding under Approved Rules that "food and arrows are not tracked as inventory resources." However, that's not strictly weapon statistics. Maybe it would fit better in a new section called "Tracking," but I wonder if it's better to leave it since the new document captures that rule. The open question about ammunition being tracked isn’t ideal. We can narrow it down to whether sling stones and crossbow bolts are treated like arrows. This makes it more accurate. I might also need to update `class-equipment-canon.md` with information about starting gear.

**Considering documentation updates**

I think I can make this more concise. In Open Questions, there might still be equipment-related queries to address, especially concerning weapons and arrows not being tracked. I should add a new document in the Source Sheets and mention it under section 23. 

Additionally, I might want to update the current conditions list to clarify how Burning/Bleeding effects work, particularly if they stack or refresh. For the Class Equipment Open Questions, it looks like starting gear issues are resolved, but I should probably adjust weapon questions regarding ammunition to be arrow-specific. Maybe a new subgroup for Mundane Equipment Open Questions would help, but it's possibly unnecessary since section 26 already organizes questions by area.

**Evaluating equipment question groups**

I could consider adding a new group called "Mundane Equipment Open Questions" with a couple of bullets about whether additional shop goods will be included later and if non-arrow ammunition like bolts or sling stones are tracked. However, the existing weapon open questions already cover ammunition, so maybe a new group isn't necessary. If section 23 has a new document, it's okay to have no mundane open questions. Since canon.md is quite large, only a few replacements are needed.

**Updating mechanics wording**

I'm considering how to word the update for Dirty Fighting, specifically about applying bleeding damage. The current wording states that when you hit, the target takes an additional die of damage equal to the damage die rolled, starting at the enemy's next turn as bleeding damage. I want to make sure this is clear and concise, so it's easy for players to understand how to use this mechanic effectively. Let's refine that wording!

**Finalizing document structure**

I need to include the note "Food and arrows are not tracked" near the top and reference the new document. I'll ensure the markdown table formatting is consistent. For the weight column, I’m contemplating whether using `0` or `—` would be better, and I think "—" might work. The weight could be numeric but can also accept text. It’s fine to change the table columns from `|---:|` to a plain `|---|`. Let's proceed to write the full file.

I want to make sure this is clear and concise, so it's easy for players to understand how to use this mechanic effectively. Let's refine that wording!"},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Finalizing document structure**\n\nI need to include the note \"Food and arrows are not tracked\" near the top and reference the new document. arguments.content:
# Umberhold Item List

This is a growing master item list for Umberhold.

It can include:
- mundane items
- magical items
- unique drops
- quest items
- consumables
- armor and weapons

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## Notes

- Settlement shop stock, hurled-item rules, and free class starting gear are tracked in `umberhold/mundane-equipment-canon.md`.
- Food and arrows are **not** tracked.

---

## Items

| Name | Type | Magical | Cost | Weight | Effect | Source / Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Boggart Cloak** | Cloak / Armor Accessory | No | — | 1 stone | Grants advantage on Stealth checks | Rare unique drop from **Boggart Knifer** (1 in 500). A patchwork cloak of filthy cloth, flayed skin, and scraps of wulf fur, reeking of damp rot. |
| **Bone Collar** | Collar / Trade Good | No | 25 Gold Crowns | TBD | No confirmed gameplay effect | Rare drop from **Wulf** (1 in 25). A crude bone collar carved by Boggart masters, etched with jagged symbols. |
| **Drums** | Instrument | No | 6 Gold Crowns (`6 gp`) | 3 stones | Counts as an instrument for Troubadour features that require one in inventory | Mundane instrument. May be chosen as a Troubadour's free starting instrument. |
| **Flask of Oil** | Consumable / Fuel / Hurled Item | No | 1 Silver Rook (`1 sp`) | 1 stone | Fills one hooded lantern for **4 hours**; may also be hurled at **range 3** for **2d4 fire** damage and **Burning** | Equipment shop stock. Automatically consumed when a hooded lantern is lit. |
| **Flute** | Instrument | No | 2 Gold Crowns (`2 gp`) | 1 stone | Counts as an instrument for Troubadour features that require one in inventory | Mundane instrument. May be chosen as a Troubadour's free starting instrument. |
| **Holy Symbol of Solace** | Religious Item | No | 5 Gold Crowns (`5 gp`) | no weight | Required for **Turn Undead**; granted free to Friars at character creation | Temple stock. |
| **Holy Water** | Consumable / Hurled Item | No | 25 Gold Crowns (`25 gp`) | 1 stone | Hurled at **range 3**; deals **2d6 holy** damage to **undead only** | Temple stock. |
| **Hooded Lantern** | Adventuring Gear / Light Source | No | 5 Gold Crowns (`5 gp`) | 2 stones | When equipped in the main hand or off-hand, provides **3 tiles of illumination**. Burns **4 hours** per flask of oil. | Equipment shop stock. Lighting the lantern automatically consumes oil from the carrier first, then a random other party member if needed. If the party has no oil: **"You have no oil to fill the lantern."** When it expires: **"Your lantern has gone out."** |
| **Horn** | Instrument | No | 3 Gold Crowns (`3 gp`) | 2 stones | Counts as an instrument for Troubadour features that require one in inventory | Mundane instrument. May be chosen as a Troubadour's free starting instrument. |
| **Lute** | Instrument | No | 35 Gold Crowns (`35 gp`) | 2 stones | Counts as an instrument for Troubadour features that require one in inventory | Mundane instrument. May be chosen as a Troubadour's free starting instrument. |

arguments.content:
# Umberhold Mundane Equipment Canon

This document captures the current approved rules for:
- mundane settlement gear
- free class starting equipment
- hurled combat items
- special rules for specific mundane items

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. General Rules

Mundane items are commonly available in settlements.

### Shop Categories
- **Equipment Shops** stock general adventuring goods in addition to weapons and armor.
- **Temples** stock items of a religious nature.

### Measurement and Tracking
- item weight is measured in **stones**
- costs below use current Umberhold coin names, with legacy D&D-style abbreviations preserved in parentheses for reference
- food is **not** tracked
- arrows are **not** tracked

---

## 2. Equipment Shop Stock

| Item | Cost | Weight | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Torch** | 1 Silver Rook (`1 sp`) | 1 stone | Common handheld light source |
| **Hooded Lantern** | 5 Gold Crowns (`5 gp`) | 2 stones | Handheld lantern fueled by oil |
| **Flask of Oil** | 1 Silver Rook (`1 sp`) | 1 stone | Lantern fuel; may also be hurled in combat |
| **Thieves' Tools** | 25 Gold Crowns (`25 gp`) | 1 stone | Required for Shadowman lock and trap work |
| **Magnifying Glass** | 100 Gold Crowns (`100 gp`) | no weight | Grants a passive trap-detection bonus |

---

## 3. Temple Stock

| Item | Cost | Weight | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Holy Water** | 25 Gold Crowns (`25 gp`) | 1 stone | May be hurled in combat; effective only against undead |
| **Holy Symbol of Solace** | 5 Gold Crowns (`5 gp`) | no weight | Required for Friar **Turn Undead** |

---

## 4. Troubadour Instruments

The following mundane instruments may be purchased normally.
A Troubadour receives **one instrument of choice for free** at character creation.

| Instrument | Cost | Weight |
|---|---|---|
| **Flute** | 2 Gold Crowns (`2 gp`) | 1 stone |
| **Lute** | 35 Gold Crowns (`35 gp`) | 2 stones |
| **Horn** | 3 Gold Crowns (`3 gp`) | 2 stones |
| **Drums** | 6 Gold Crowns (`6 gp`) | 3 stones |
| **Pipes** | 12 Gold Crowns (`12 gp`) | 1 stone |

---

## 5. Hurled Combat Items

Certain mundane items may be used as hurled weapons in combat.

### Hurled Attack Rule
When a hurled mundane item requires an attack roll:
- roll **`d20 + Agility bonus`**
- compare the result against the target's **AC**
- the attack has **range 3**

The item's listed on-hit effect then resolves normally.

---

## 6. Mundane Item Combat Effects

### Holy Water
- **range 3**
- deals **`2d6` holy damage**
- affects **undead only**

### Flask of Oil
- **range 3**
- on hit: deals **`2d4` fire damage**
- on hit: applies **Burning**
- at the start of the target's next turn: deals **`1d4` fire damage**

---

## 7. Free Starting Equipment

At character creation, the following classes begin with free mundane gear:

- **Friar** — **Holy Symbol of Solace**
- **Shadowman** — **Thieves' Tools**
- **Troubadour** — **one instrument of choice** from the approved mundane instrument list

---

## 8. Special Item Rules

### Magnifying Glass
- grants **`+2` to detect traps** while in inventory
- does **not** need to be equipped

---

## 9. Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- mundane settlement stock is divided between equipment shops and temples
- weights for these items use stones
- food and arrows are not tracked
- torch, hooded lantern, flask of oil, thieves' tools, and magnifying glass are standard equipment-shop stock
- holy water and holy symbols of Solace are standard temple stock
- Troubadours start with one mundane instrument of choice
- Friars start with a holy symbol of Solace
- Shadowmen start with thieves' tools
- hurled mundane combat items use **`d20 + Agility bonus vs AC`**
- hurled mundane combat items have **range 3** unless a specific item says otherwise
- holy water deals **`2d6` holy damage** to undead only
- flasks of oil deal **`2d4` fire damage** on hit and apply **Burning** for **`1d4` fire damage** at the start of the target's next turn
- magnifying glasses grant **`+2` to detect traps** while carried and do not require equipping

---

## 10. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

arguments.content:
# Umberhold Class Hit Dice and Equipment Canon

This document captures the current approved rules for:
- class hit dice per level
- armor and shield restrictions
- weapon proficiencies
- non-proficient weapon use
- free class starting equipment

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. Class Table

| Class | Hit Points / Level | Armor / Shield | Weapon Proficiencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Shadowman** | d8 | Light armor, no shield | All single-handed melee weapons, shortbow, hand crossbow |
| **Man-at-Arms** | d10 | Any armor, can equip shield | All weapons |
| **Warden** | d10 | Light to medium armor, can equip shield | All single-handed melee weapons, shortbow, longbow, boar spear |
| **Hedge Wizard** | d6 | No armor, no shield | Quarterstaff, dagger |
| **Friar** | d8 | Any armor, can equip shield | All bludgeoning weapons |
| **Crusader** | d10 | Any armor, can equip shield | All weapons |
| **Troubadour** | d8 | Light armor, no shield | All single-handed melee weapons |

---

## 2. Hit Points Per Level

A character's hit points are determined by their class hit die.

### Hit Point Roll Rule
At each level:
- roll the class hit die
- add the character's **Constitution modifier** to the roll

### No Average Option
There is **no average hit point option**.
Every level requires an actual hit point roll.

---

## 3. Armor Restrictions

Armor restrictions are **absolute**.

If a class cannot equip a given armor type, it cannot wear that armor under any circumstances.

### Inventory Handling
A character may still carry unusable armor, but it should be displayed in **red text** in the inventory to show that it cannot be equipped by that class.

---

## 4. Shield Restrictions

Shield restrictions follow the class table exactly.

If a class cannot equip a shield, it cannot use one under any circumstances.

---

## 5. Weapon Proficiency Rules

Any class may equip any weapon.

However, the **Proficiency Bonus** is only applied to attack rolls when the class is proficient with that weapon.

### Non-Proficient Weapon Rule
If a class uses a weapon it is not proficient with:
- the attack may still be made
- the attack roll does **not** add the Proficiency Bonus

---

## 6. Free Starting Equipment

At character creation, the following classes begin with free gear:

- **Friar** — **Holy Symbol of Solace**
- **Shadowman** — **Thieves' Tools**
- **Troubadour** — **one mundane instrument of choice** from the approved instrument list

These items are granted in addition to any other character-creation systems that may later be approved.

---

## 7. Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- each class has a defined hit die per level
- Constitution modifier is added to each hit point roll
- every level requires a hit point roll
- there is no average hit point option
- armor restrictions are absolute
- shield restrictions are absolute
- unusable armor may still be carried but should display in red in inventory
- any class may equip any weapon
- weapon proficiency only affects whether Proficiency Bonus is added to attack rolls
- non-proficient weapon attacks do not gain Proficiency Bonus
- Friars start with a **Holy Symbol of Solace**
- Shadowmen start with **Thieves' Tools**
- Troubadours start with **one mundane instrument of choice**

---

## 8. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- whether first-level characters still begin with maximum hit points from their class hit die
- whether off-hand weapon proficiency follows the same rule with no exceptions
- whether class proficiencies can ever be expanded by training, items, or special features
- whether instruments for Troubadours count as weapon-category items for equipment UI purposes
- whether some relic or class effects can temporarily bypass armor restrictions

---

## 9. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

- whether instruments for Troubadours count as weapon-category items for equipment UI purposes
- whether some relic or class effects can temporarily bypass armor restrictions

---

## 9. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

arguments.content:
# Umberhold Conditions Canon

This document captures the current approved condition list for Umberhold.

These conditions may still be tuned later, but this file records the current working canon.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## 1. Condition List

### Beguiled
The target is confused, takes no hostile actions, and does not move.

Rules:
- the target does not take hostile actions
- the target does not move
- the condition ends immediately if the target takes damage

---

### Bleeding
The target is suffering a damage-over-time wound.

Rules:
- **Bleeding** is a damage-over-time effect
- the source that applied **Bleeding** defines the damage amount
- the source that applied **Bleeding** also defines the damage type, if relevant
- unless a source says otherwise, the damage is taken at the **start of the target's next turn**
- after that damage resolves, **Bleeding** ends unless the source says it lasts longer

---

### Blinded
The target cannot see.

Rules:
- attacks against the target have **advantage**
- attacks made by the target have **disadvantage**

---

### Burning
The target is on fire and suffers damage over time.

Rules:
- **Burning** is a damage-over-time effect
- the source that applied **Burning** defines the damage amount
- **Burning** damage is normally **fire** damage unless a source explicitly says otherwise
- unless a source says otherwise, the damage is taken at the **start of the target's next turn**
- after that damage resolves, **Burning** ends unless the source says it lasts longer

---

### Frightened
The target is overcome by fear.

Rules:
- the target has **disadvantage** on attacks
- the target cannot willingly move forward on the combat grid

---

### Paralyzed
The target is physically locked in place.

Rules:
- the target cannot take actions
- the target cannot move
- attacks against the target have **advantage**
- melee attacks against the target are automatic **critical hits**
- the target automatically fails **Strength** and **Agility** saving throws

---

### Poisoned
The target is suffering from poison.

Rules:
- the target has **disadvantage** on attacks
- the target has **disadvantage** on ability checks

---

### Silenced
The target cannot produce speech-based magical effects.

Rules:
- the target cannot cast spells that require speech
- the target cannot use abilities that require speech
- this includes songs and similar vocal performances

---

### Slept
The target is in unnatural sleep.

Rules:
- the target cannot take actions
- the target cannot move
- attacks against the target have **advantage**
- melee attacks against the target are automatic **critical hits**
- the target automatically fails **Strength** and **Agility** saving throws
- the target wakes immediately if it takes damage

---

### Stunned
The target is dazed and unable to act normally.

Rules:
- attacks against the target have **advantage**
- the target has **disadvantage** on attacks
- the target has **disadvantage** on **Strength** and **Agility** saving throws

---

## 2. Current Condition Summary

The current approved condition list is:
- Beguiled
- Bleeding
- Blinded
- Burning
- Frightened
- Paralyzed
- Poisoned
- Silenced
- Slept
- Stunned

---

## 3. Notes on Current Canon

These conditions are currently captured as approved working rules.

Three implementation notes remain worth reviewing later:
- Umberhold currently does **not** use reactions, so any reaction-denial text is unnecessary under current canon.
- Player characters do not normally move during combat, so movement-restriction conditions may matter primarily for enemies unless later mechanics expand party movement.
- **Burning** and **Bleeding** are source-defined damage-over-time conditions, with the current default pattern being damage at the start of the target's next turn.

These are not blockers, but they are useful for future cleanup.

---

## 4. Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- whether Beguiled and Soothed should be separate conditions or the same rules concept
- whether Stunned should also prevent main actions entirely
- whether Frightened affects spell targeting or only attacks
- whether Blinded affects spellcasting for targeted spells
- whether multiple **Burning** or **Bleeding** applications stack, refresh, or overwrite one another
- whether condition durations always expire at start or end of turn depending on source
- whether bosses can be immune to some control conditions by default
- whether there are cleanse rules tied to items, spells, or rest

---

## 5. Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

arguments.oldText:
### 5.3 Use an Item
A combatant may use an item from their inventory, such as:
- a potion
- a curative item
- another usable combat item

Item target and effect rules depend on the item being used.
arguments.newText:
### 5.3 Use an Item
A combatant may use an item from their inventory, such as:
- a potion
- a curative item
- another usable combat item

Item target and effect rules depend on the item being used.

#### Hurled Combat Items
Some items may be used as **hurled combat items**, such as **Holy Water** or a **Flask of Oil**.

When a hurled item requires an attack roll:
- roll **`d20 + Agility Bonus`**
- compare the result against the target's **AC**
- the hurled attack has **range 3** unless the item says otherwise
- on a hit, resolve the item's listed effect

### Dirty Fighting
When you hit, the target takes one additional die of damage—matching the damage die you rolled—at the start of the enemy's next turn as **bleeding** damage.
arguments.newText:
### Dirty Fighting
When you hit, you apply **Bleeding**.
At the start of the target's next turn, it takes one additional die of damage matching the damage die you rolled.

arguments.oldText:
## 23. Class Hit Dice and Equipment Rules

The current approved class hit-die and equipment rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/class-equipment-canon.md`

### Current Class Equipment Rules
- each class has a defined hit die per level
- Constitution modifier is added to each hit point roll
- every level requires a hit point roll
- there is no average hit point option
- armor restrictions are absolute
- shield restrictions are absolute
- unusable armor may still be carried but should display in red in inventory
- any class may equip any weapon
- weapon proficiency only affects whether Proficiency Bonus is added to attack rolls
- non-proficient weapon attacks do not gain Proficiency Bonus

---

## 24. Weapon Statistics
arguments.newText:
## 23. Mundane and Class Equipment Rules

The current approved mundane equipment, settlement stock, and free starting gear rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/mundane-equipment-canon.md`

The current approved class hit-die and equipment restriction rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/class-equipment-canon.md`

### Current Mundane Equipment Rules
- mundane settlement stock is divided between equipment shops and temples
- food and arrows are not tracked
- certain items may be hurled with `d20 + Agility Bonus vs AC` at range 3
- holy water deals `2d6` holy damage to undead only
- flasks of oil deal `2d4` fire damage on hit and apply Burning for `1d4` fire damage at the start of the target's next turn
- Friars start with a Holy Symbol of Solace
- Shadowmen start with Thieves' Tools
- Troubadours start with one mundane instrument of choice
- magnifying glasses grant `+2` to detect traps while carried and do not need to be equipped

### Current Class Equipment Rules
- each class has a defined hit die per level
- Constitution modifier is added to each hit point roll
- every level requires a hit point roll
- there is no average hit point option
- armor restrictions are absolute
- shield restrictions are absolute
- unusable armor may still be carried but should display in red in inventory
- any class may equip any weapon
- weapon proficiency only affects whether Proficiency Bonus is added to attack rolls
- non-proficient weapon attacks do not gain Proficiency Bonus

---

## 24. Weapon Statistics

arguments.oldText:
- reactions do not exist
- Defend imposes disadvantage on incoming attacks
- weapon swapping uses the full main action
- Troubadours may begin or maintain Songs with a **bonus action** when granted by class features
- extra attacks cannot be redirected if the original target dies
arguments.newText:
- reactions do not exist
- Defend imposes disadvantage on incoming attacks
- weapon swapping uses the full main action
- hurled combat items use **`d20 + Agility Bonus vs AC`** and normally have **range 3**

arguments.oldText:
### Currency and Gemstone Rules
- the current currency rules are tracked in `umberhold/currency-canon.md`
- the current gemstone reference is tracked in `umberhold/items/gemstones.md`
- Silver Rook is the base coin
- Gold Crown is worth 10 Silver Rooks
- Platinum Stag is worth 25 Gold Crowns

### Class Equipment Rules
arguments.newText:
### Currency and Gemstone Rules
- the current currency rules are tracked in `umberhold/currency-canon.md`
- the current gemstone reference is tracked in `umberhold/items/gemstones.md`
- Silver Rook is the base coin
- Gold Crown is worth 10 Silver Rooks
- Platinum Stag is worth 25 Gold Crowns

### Mundane Equipment Rules
- the current mundane equipment sheet is tracked in `umberhold/mundane-equipment-canon.md`
- mundane settlement stock is divided between equipment shops and temples
- food and arrows are not tracked
- holy water deals `2d6` holy damage to undead only
- flasks of oil deal `2d4` fire damage on hit and apply Burning for `1d4` fire damage at the start of the target's next turn
- Friars start with a **Holy Symbol of Solace**
- Shadowmen start with **Thieves' Tools**
- Troubadours start with **one mundane instrument of choice**
- magnifying glasses grant `+2` to detect traps while carried and do not require equipping

### Class Equipment Rules

arguments.oldText:
## 5. Hooded Lantern

A hooded lantern burns for **4 hours** on **one vial of oil**.

Under the shared time system, this equals **2,400 time steps**.

### Fuel Use
When a lantern is lit:
- oil is first taken automatically from the carrying character's inventory
- if the carrier has no oil, one vial is taken from a **random other party member**
- if the party has no oil, display:
arguments.newText:

A hooded lantern burns for **4 hours** on **one flask of oil**.

Under the shared time system, this equals **2,400 time steps**.

### Fuel Use
When a lantern is lit:
- oil is first taken automatically from the carrying character's inventory
- if the carrier has no oil, one flask is taken from a **random other party member**
- if the party has no oil, display:
